core_model.structure_summary: A leader-follower strategic interaction in which one agent maximizes a discounted revenue objective over a bounded control while followers select among options to maximize private utilities, coupled to a temporal-difference (Q-learning) fixed-point update over the leader's control-value function.
core_model.canonical_form: \max_{\{p^t\}}\ \sum_{t}\gamma^t r_t\ \ \text{s.t. partition is Nash-stable},\ p_{\min}\le p^t\le p_{\max};\quad Q\leftarrow Q+\alpha\big(r_t+\gamma\max_{a'}Q'-Q\big)

variables.meaning: occupancy/congestion and remaining-charge descriptors of agents and options
bounded price set by the revenue-maximizing leader
discounted cumulative reward and per-agent utility functions
discount factor, learning rate, utility weights, and bounds

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A constrained bilevel optimization: maximize a discounted cumulative objective over a bounded control subject to an equilibrium (Nash-stable assignment) constraint among self-interested followers.

notes: Composite paper: a game-based follower-selection model (Nash-stable partition with utility functions), a leader revenue maximization, and a Q-learning update to solve it. Treated the game-plus-bilevel-control structure as core; the deterministic skeleton is the constrained bilevel optimization with an equilibrium constraint, stripping the stochastic RL solver. Equations are noisy (piecewise waiting-time term, utility form); confidence moderate.
